English
Division of Rare and Manuscript Collections
Homo Xtra (HX) records
Bank, Matthew
7840
4.2 cubic feet. 4.2 cubic feet.
circa 1991-2009. Printed materials, media, manuscripts. Organizational files from the co-publisher Matthew Blank. Collection material in English 9945121
General
Contact Information:
Division of Rare and Manuscript Collections 2B Carl A. Kroch Library Cornell University Ithaca, NY 14853 (607) 255-3530 Fax: (607) 255-9524rareref@cornell.eduhttp://rmc.library.cornell.edu
Compiled by:
RMC Staff
Date completed:
February 2018
EAD encoding:
Jude Corina, February 2018
BIOGRAPHICAL NOTE
A weekly gay entertainment guide for New York City, 1991 to July 10, 2009, HX, titled Homo Xtra for a while, was available for free in bars and other locations, concentrated in the West Village and Chelsea and, during the summer, in the Pines and Cherry Grove. Its slogan was: "The totally biased, politically incorrect party paper".
COLLECTION DESCRIPTION
Organizational files from the co-publisher Matthew Blank.
RELATED MATERIALS
Periodicals cataloged separately as part of the Human Sexuality Collection.
Cite As:
Homo Xtra (HX) records, #7840. Division of Rare and Manuscript Collections, Cornell University Library.
Restrictions on Use:
Due to the fragility and potential degradation of moving image and sound recordings, viewing and listening is limited to items that have been digitized. If an item is in another media format, you may request to have the item digitized for access. Information on ordering access copies may be found on our Reproductions & Permissions page.
Gay men -- Social life and customs -- Periodicals. Gay men -- New York -- Periodicals.
